sections.title: 2022 Amendments
sections.text: Following decisions taken at the Forty-fifth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2022, amendments were made in Section 8.2 Labelling of non-retail containers.

page_spans.text: Following decisions taken at the Forty-fifth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2022, amendments were made in Section 8.2 Labelling of non-retail containers.

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard applies to canned applesauce, as defined in Section 2 below, and offered for direct consumption, including for catering purposes or for repacking if required. It does not apply to the product when indicated as being intended for further processing.
sections.retrieval_text:

number=1
level=1

sections.title: Product definition
sections.text:
Canned applesauce is the comminuted or chopped product:
a) prepared from washed, clean apples, conforming to the characteristics of the fruit of Malus domesticus Borkhausen which may have been peeled and which after trimming are sound;
b) prepared with or without other permitted ingredients as described in Section 3.1.2; and
c) processed by heat, in an appropriate manner, before or after being hermetically sealed in a container, so as to prevent spoilage.

page_spans.text:
Canned applesauce is the comminuted or chopped product:
a) prepared from washed, clean apples, conforming to the characteristics of the fruit of Malus domesticus Borkhausen which may have been peeled and which after trimming are sound;
b) prepared with or without other permitted ingredients as described in Section 3.1.2; and
c) processed by heat, in an appropriate manner, before or after being hermetically sealed in a container, so as to prevent spoilage.

sections.title: Classification of “defectives”
sections.text: A container that fails to meet one or more of the applicable quality requirements, or total solids requirements, as set out in Section 2.2, should be considered as a “defective”.
sections.retrieval_text:
ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS > Classification of “defectives”
number=3.3
level=2
A container that fails to meet one or more of the applicable quality requirements, or total solids requirements, as set out in Section 2.2, should be considered as a “defective”.

page_spans.text: A container that fails to meet one or more of the applicable quality requirements, or total solids requirements, as set out in Section 2.2, should be considered as a “defective”.

sections.title: Lot acceptance
sections.text: A lot should be considered as meeting the applicable quality requirements for total solids referred to in Section 2.2, and the quality criteria in Section 3.2, when the number of “defectives” does not exceed the acceptance number c) of an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5.

sections.title: Other styles
sections.text:
Any other presentation of the product should be permitted provided that the product:
a) is sufficiently distinctive from other forms of presentation laid down in the standard;
b) meets all other requirements of the standard, as applicable; and
c) is adequately described on the label to avoid confusing or misleading the consumer.

sections.title: Other permitted ingredients
Colour, flavour and texture
sections.text: Applesauce shall have normal colour, flavour and odour and shall possess a texture characteristic of the product. The number, size, and prominence of defects (such as seeds or particles thereof, peel, carpel tissue, bruised apple particles, dark particles, and any other extraneous material of like nature) should not seriously affect the appearance or the eating quality of the product.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text:
5.1 The product covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for 
Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).1 
5.2 The product covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established 
by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text:
6.1 It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in 
accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969)2 and other 
relevant Codex texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice.  
6.2 The product should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and 
Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).3

sections.title: Name of the product
sections.text:
8.1.1  The name of the product shall be “Applesauce”. 
8.1.2  If the product has been sweetened in accordance with the provisions of Section 2.2.1, the name of the product 
shall be accompanied by the designation “Sweetened”, in close proximity to the name of the food.  
8.1.3  If the product has not been sweetened and  conforms with the requirements of Section 2.2.2, the designation 
“Unsweetened” may be used in close proximity to the name of the food.

sections.title: Minimum fill
sections.text:
The container should be well filled with applesauce and the product should occupy not less than 90 percent of 
the water capacity of the container. The water capacity of the container is the volume of distilled water at 20 oC 
which the sealed container will hold when completely filled.

sections.title: Classification of “defectives”
sections.text:
A container that fails to meet the requirement for minimum fill (90 percent container capacity) of Section 7.1.1 
should be considered as a “defective”.

sections.title: Lot acceptance
sections.text:
A lot should be considered as meeting the requirement of Section 7.1.1 when the number of “defectives”, as 
defined in Section 7.1.2, does not exceed the acceptance number c) of an appropriate sampling plan with an 
AQL of 6.5.

sections.title: Labelling of non-retail containers
sections.text: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
sections.retrieval_text:
8.2 Labelling of non-retail containers
number=8.2
level=1
The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

page_spans.text: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

sections.title: METHODS OF ANALYSIS AND SAMPLING
sections.text: For checking compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard shall be used.
sections.retrieval_text:
9 METHODS OF ANALYSIS AND SAMPLING
number=9
level=1
For checking compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard shall be used.

page_spans.text: For checking compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard shall be used.

sections.title: NOTES
sections.text:
1 FAO and WHO. 1995. General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed. Codex Alimentarius Standard, No. CXS 193-1995. Codex Alimentarius Commission. Rome. 
2 FAO and WHO. 1969. General Principles of Food Hygiene . Codex Alimentarius Code of Practice, No. CXC 1-1969. Codex Alimentarius Commission. Rome. 
3 FAO and WHO. 1997. Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria related to Foods. Codex Alimentarius Guideline, No. CXG 21-1997. Codex Alimentarius Commission. Rome. 
4 FAO and WHO. 1985. General Standard for the Labelling of Pre -packaged Foods. Codex Alimentarius Standard, No. CXS 1-1985. Codex Alimentarius Commission. Rome. 
5 FAO and WHO. 2021. General Standard for the Labelling of Non -Retail Containers of Foods . Codex Alimentarius Standard, No. CXS 346-2021 Codex Alimentarius Commission. Rome. 
6 FAO and WHO. 1999. Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling. Codex Alimentarius Standard, No. CXS 234-1999. Codex Alimentarius Commission. Rome.

sentences.original: Following decisions taken at the Forty-fifth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2022, amendments were made in Section 8.2 Labelling of non-retail containers.

prefilter.reason: Contains relevant regulatory decision information.

classification.sentence: Following decisions taken at the Forty-fifth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2022, amendments were made in Section 8.2 Labelling of non-retail containers.

notes: No regulatory modality or direct obligation/permission/prohibition present.

sentences.original: This standard applies to canned applesauce, as defined in Section 2 below, and offered for direct consumption, including for catering purposes or for repacking if required.

prefilter.reason: Contains a regulatory exemption statement.

classification.sentence: It does not apply to the product when indicated as being intended for further processing.
classification.justification: The sentence expresses a conditional negation on applicability, a scope restriction.

sentences.original:
Canned applesauce is the comminuted or chopped product:
a) prepared from washed, clean apples, conforming to the characteristics of the fruit of Malus domesticus Borkhausen which may have been peeled and which after trimming are sound;
b) prepared with or without other permitted ingredients as described in Section 3.1.2; and
c) processed by heat, in an appropriate manner, before or after being hermetically sealed in a container, so as to prevent spoilage.

prefilter.reason: contains normative lexical cues

classification.sentence:
Canned applesauce is the comminuted or chopped product:
a) prepared from washed, clean apples, conforming to the characteristics of the fruit of Malus domesticus Borkhausen which may have been peeled and which after trimming are sound;
b) prepared with or without other permitted ingredients as described in Section 3.1.2; and
c) processed by heat, in an appropriate manner, before or after being hermetically sealed in a container, so as to prevent spoilage.
classification.function: defines product characteristics and preparation requirements
classification.pattern: main clause with enumerated subclauses describing preparation and composition

characteristics: may have been peeled and trimmed
prepared with or without other permitted ingredients (Section 3.1.2)
processed by heat before or after hermetic sealing to prevent spoilage

classification.justification: Complex multi-part definition with detailed preparation steps and composition, no exact template match.
notes: Detailed enumeration and product definition pattern differing from simpler definitions.

sentences.original: A container that fails to meet one or more of the applicable quality requirements, or total solids requirements, as set out in Section 2.2, should be considered as a “defective”.

prefilter.reason: contains normative lexical cues

classification.sentence: A container that fails to meet one or more of the applicable quality requirements, or total solids requirements, as set out in Section 2.2, should be considered as a “defective”.

components.condition: container fails to meet quality or total solids requirements (Section 2.2)

classification.justification: Conditional classification based on failure to meet criteria; no strong template match but clear condition and decision.
notes: Condition and consequence pattern often seen but no matching template available.

sentences.original: A lot should be considered as meeting the applicable quality requirements for total solids referred to in Section 2.2, and the quality criteria in Section 3.2, when the number of “defectives” does not exceed the acceptance number c) of an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5.

prefilter.reason: contains normative lexical cues

classification.sentence: A lot should be considered as meeting the applicable quality requirements for total solids referred to in Section 2.2, and the quality criteria in Section 3.2, when the number of “defectives” does not exceed the acceptance number c) of an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5.
classification.function: sets acceptance criteria based on sampling results
classification.pattern: conditional statement relating defectives count to acceptance

components.condition: number of 'defectives' does not exceed acceptance number c) of sampling plan with AQL 6.5

classification.justification: Acceptance decision conditioned on sampling plan results; normative condition with no exact template match.
notes: Complex conditional acceptance clause involving sampling plans and AQL.

sentences.original:
Any other presentation of the product should be permitted provided that the product:
a) is sufficiently distinctive from other forms of presentation laid down in the standard;
b) meets all other requirements of the standard, as applicable; and
c) is adequately described on the label to avoid confusing or misleading the consumer.

prefilter.reason: contains normative lexical cues

classification.sentence:
Any other presentation of the product should be permitted provided that the product:  
a) is sufficiently distinctive from other forms of presentation laid down in the standard; 
b) meets all other requirements of the standard, as applicable; and 
c) is adequately described on the label to avoid confusing or misleading the consumer.

components.permission_condition: Any other presentation of the product should be permitted
conditions: distinctiveness from other forms of presentation
adequately labeled to avoid consumer confusion

classification.justification: Permission modality qualified by three explicit conditions presented in an enumerated structure.

sentences.original: Applesauce shall have normal colour, flavour and odour and shall possess a texture characteristic of the product.

prefilter.reason: contains normative lexical cues

classification.sentence: The number, size, and prominence of defects (such as seeds or particles thereof, peel, carpel tissue, bruised apple particles, dark particles, and any other extraneous material of like nature) should not seriously affect the appearance or the eating quality of the product.
classification.function: limits negative effect of defects on product quality
classification.pattern: negative obligation statement with explanatory parenthetical examples

components.tolerance: should not seriously affect appearance or eating quality

classification.justification: Normative sentence limiting tolerance of visible/eating quality defects using modal 'should'; no exact template.
notes: Negative obligation with detailed defect enumeration.

sentences.original:
5.1 The product covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for 
Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).1 
5.2 The product covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established 
by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

prefilter.reason: contains normative lexical cues

classification.sentence:
5.1 The product covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for 
Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).1 
5.2 The product covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established 
by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.
classification.pattern: compound sentences with modal 'shall comply' referencing external standards

requirements: comply with max levels in General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins (CXS 193-1995)
comply with max residue limits for pesticides by Codex Alimentarius Commission

classification.justification: Obligatory compliance statements referencing external standards; common regulatory pattern but no catalog template found.
notes: Two similar normative clauses combined.

sentences.original:
6.1 It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in 
accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969)2 and other 
relevant Codex texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice.

prefilter.reason: contains normative lexical cues

classification.sentence:
6.2 The product should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and 
Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).3
classification.pattern: modal 'should' statement requiring compliance with criteria

components.requirement: product should comply with microbiological criteria established per guidelines (CXG 21-1997)

classification.justification: Modal verb 'should' signals a normative advice or soft obligation; no exact template match.
notes: Some ambiguity between obligation and recommendation.

classification.sentence: In addition, the following specific provisions apply:
classification.pattern: Introductory phrase + declarative statement indicating applicability of provisions
classification.justification: The sentence imposes an obligation by stating that certain provisions apply. Its structure is an introductory phrase followed by a declarative statement. Since no known templates exist and the sentence does not fit typical modal verbs or conditional structures, it is classified as a new pattern with obligation modality and imposition communicative function.
notes: The sentence is an introductory clause serving to announce subsequent obligations, without explicit modal verbs.

The container should be well filled with applesauce and the product should occupy not less than 90 percent of 
the water capacity of the container.

sentences.original: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

prefilter.reason: contains normative lexical cues

classification.sentence: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
classification.function: Mandate to comply with a specified standard
classification.pattern: Subject + modal (should) + be + in accordance with + reference to a standard

components.subject: The labelling of non-retail containers
components.prescribed_action: be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021)

classification.justification: The sentence issues an obligation expressed with 'should' regarding compliance with a specific standard. The structure involves a subject and a compliance mandate referencing an external standard. Since no existing templates were matched and the structure is specific, it was classified as a new pattern with obligation modality and mandate communicative function.
notes: The use of 'should' here conveys a normative expectation often treated as obligation in regulatory text.
No exact template was found for 'should be in accordance with' phrasing referencing a standard.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR CANNED APPLESAUCE
pages.summary: Página com título principal do documento e cabeçalho padrão, sem subseções. Texto associado é a descrição da adoção e revisões.

Página contém um título principal em caixa alta não numerado: '2022 Amendments'. Texto explicativo relacionado está associado a este título. Não há subdivisões nesta página. Cabeçalho padrão identificado 'CXS 17-1981 2'. Conteúdo novo, não continuidade da página anterior.

A página contém três grandes seções numeradas: '1. SCOPE', '2. DESCRIPTION' (com subseções 2.1, 2.2 e subsubseções 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3) e '3. ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS' (com subseções 3.1 a 3.4 e subsubseções 3.1.1, 3.1.2 e 3.2.1). A estrutura segue a numeração hierárquica corretamente e inclui o texto explicativo direto associado a cada título, preservando a continuidade e sem indicar continuidade para outras páginas.

A página contém as seções 4 a 8 com subseções e subsubseções hierarquicamente estruturadas e associadas ao conteúdo pertinente. Cada seção está marcada com título visível e sem indicações de continuidade entre páginas.

A página contém as seções 8.1.4, 8.1.5, 8.2, 9 e a seção final NOTES. As seções 8.1.4 e 8.1.5 são continuação da página anterior, indicadas por is_continuation_from_previous_page true. O conteúdo das seções é sucinto, sem continuidade para a próxima página. O cabeçalho da página foi identificado como 'CXS 17-1981 5'. Não há título principal do documento visível nesta página.
